focus: Consensus Flow Verification - Part 2: collab bus workflow implementation
summary: Collab bus is implemented as a protocol scaffold: stampede.sh bootstraps a collab/ directory, writes protocol.json, touch-creates 5 append-only JSONL ledgers, and exports their paths to commanders. Actual workflow semantics (propose/peer_review/improve/consensus/broadcast/adopt) are not enforced by code; they are delegated to LLM commanders via prompt instructions. Monitoring only counts ledger line totals.

behavior: Write collab/protocol.json atomically with workflow sequence + required_fields + append guidance.
Export STAMPEDE_COLLAB_* env vars for commander processes pointing to the ledger paths.
Inject natural-language instructions in the commander prompt to follow propose->peer_review->improve->consensus->broadcast->adopt.
print_collab_summary() shows counts only: wc -l of each ledger; missing file => 0 lines.
No semantic validation or workflow completeness checks.
Bundle must include consensus.tier = CONSENSUS|MAJORITY|CONFLICT|UNIQUE, but no code computes it.

notes: Protocol requires fields ts, run_id, commander_id, event, item_id, summary, evidence, confidence, source_refs; not validated.
No helper to perform atomic single-line append; relies on commander tool usage.
No peer selection/balancing or anti-self-review enforcement.
No aggregation/approval logic; reviews are informational only unless agents act on them.
No iterative revision loop enforced; no “changes requested” state machine.
No guard ensuring every review/proposal is addressed by an improvement.

consensus.implemented_as: prompt + bundle contract tier field (no computation)
notes: No quorum/threshold definitions; MAJORITY vs CONSENSUS boundary undefined.
No conflict resolution workflow beyond allowing tier=CONFLICT in bundle.

broadcast.implemented_as: prompt instruction to append broadcasts when consensus should be adopted
notes: No code consumer/dispatcher; downstream adoption is manual by commanders reading broadcasts.

adopt.implemented_as: prompt instruction + inclusion in bundle.json/final result
notes: Workflow sequence includes adopt, but there is no adopt.jsonl ledger; adoption is not auditable as a first-class event.

risks_gaps.issue: No enforcement/validation of protocol required_fields or event schema; malformed/partial JSON lines can silently break collaboration.
No guaranteed atomic JSONL append implementation; protocol says O_APPEND semantics but provides no shared writer; concurrent writes risk interleaving/corruption.
No consensus algorithm (thresholds/quorum/voting). Consensus tier is subjective agent output only.
No adopt ledger despite workflow listing adopt; adoption state only appears in bundles if agents comply.
collab.counts in state.json is initialized to zeros and never updated (not useful telemetry).
state.json update in stampede.sh is a read-modify-write without lock; errors are swallowed (possible lost updates).

evidence: tests/smoke-test.sh validates metaswarm structured contract (fleet.json, swarm-state.json, child-agents.jsonl) but does not validate collab ledgers.
No tests found for collab/protocol.json schema, JSONL writer safety, or end-to-end propose->adopt flow.

actionable_recommendations: Add a small, shared “jsonl_append_atomic” helper (python3) used by commanders/workers to guarantee single-line append + newline + fsync when needed.
Add a tolerant JSONL reader helper that ignores malformed trailing lines, and use it in any automated consumers/monitors.
Add minimal schema validation (required fields + event type) in monitor and/or in a validator script run at end-of-run.
Either add adopt.jsonl ledger or remove adopt from declared workflow to match implementation.
Define consensus thresholds/quorum rules in protocol.json (even if still agent-executed) to reduce ambiguity.
